decision: Math/libm, CSS hyphenation/text layout, WebAudio backing-array semantics, and WASM/JS numeric parity are explicit non-release-grade surfaces until local oracle probes and target-platform evidence exist. The current fix path is policy-gated; no native Chromium/V8 patch should be claimed for these surfaces from detector summaries alone.

blocked_by: No committed d8 oracle result for target OS/CPU math output
No selected V8/libm patch target in the Chromium M150 checkout
No detector or BrowserForge runtime surface currently observes Math.tanh/CSS trig parity
gates.decision: Do not claim OS math/libm parity for a persona until a d8 oracle captures exact target-platform Math.tanh and CSS trig behavior, and any native patch is paired with detector evidence.

probe.minimum_oracle: Exact IEEE-754 outputs for Math.tanh and CSS trig-equivalent values on each claimed target OS/CPU/browser build.
probe.probe_body: Evaluate bounded Math.tanh, sin/cos/tan/atan2, and CSS trig-equivalent cases in the selected runtime and compare raw double/serialization output against a target-platform oracle.

blocked_by: No content_shell or Chrome text-layout oracle for target OS language dictionaries
No Chromium/Blink hyphenation patch target is selected
Existing font allowlist does not alter line breaking, dictionary selection, glyph fallback, or metrics
gates.decision: Treat CSS hyphenation and text layout as font/text-layout parity risk, not as fixed by FontFaceSet.check allowlists. Do not promote cross-OS text layout parity without a target dictionary and rendered layout oracle.

probe.minimum_oracle: Per-language hyphenation break positions, line boxes, and glyph metric hashes for claimed target OS/browser/font corpus.
probe.probe_body: Render controlled hyphenation samples with lang-specific CSS hyphens:auto, collect break positions and layout metrics, and compare them with target OS/browser oracle data.

blocked_by: Existing audio noise patch changes getChannelData object/backing-array semantics unless proven safe
No committed local semantics probe compares repeated getChannelData identity and mutation visibility with audio noise enabled
Patch-best-practice audit requires strict native suppression until semantics are fixed or proven
gates.decision: Keep strict native mode suppressing active audio noise and do not use AudioContext detector hash stability as proof of native AudioBuffer object/backing-array semantics.

probe.minimum_oracle: With noise enabled and disabled, repeated AudioBuffer.getChannelData calls preserve native object identity expectations and mutation visibility for dense, sparse, and uniform buffers.
probe.probe_body: Create AudioBuffer samples, call getChannelData repeatedly, mutate returned arrays, and compare identity/mutation behavior plus copyFromChannel offsets against unmodified Chromium and patched BrowseForge Chromium.

blocked_by: No d8 availability/status is recorded in source-acquisition metadata
No V8/WASM relaxed-SIMD or NaN-bit oracle exists for target CPU families
No WASM/WebAssembly patchset, runtime surface, detector row, or KG surface is present
gates.decision: Do not claim WASM/JS numeric CPU parity or Apple/x86 identity coherence until d8 probes establish NaN-bit, typed-array, scalar, and relaxed-SIMD behavior against target hardware or an accepted unsupported-persona policy.

probe.minimum_oracle: Raw JS typed-array NaN payload bits, WASM scalar NaN bits, relaxed SIMD lane behavior, and architecture-specific outputs for each claimed CPU family.
probe.probe_body: Run d8 mjsunit-style JS/WASM probes for NaN payload canonicalization, quiet/signaling NaN behavior, typed-array round trips, scalar operations, and relaxed-SIMD edge cases.
